markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Close parity rows ED-NAV-004 (prose document outline panel, heading-tree, click-to-scroll), ED-WB-007 (status bar: cursor position, language of focused code block, save/dirty/conflict state, word count), ED-NAV-006 (go-to-line inside focused code block via palette), EXT-SAVE-001 (Mod-s save command + palette entry), EXT-NAV-LINK-001 (hsLink click navigation: typed links open their real target surface; unresolvable -> typed error, never silent). Assembles onto the existing RichTextEditor/toolbar/palette from MT-169/170.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.
Bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries only (Tiptap/Monaco precedent); no CDN; no external app launches as product behavior.
PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; UI artifacts are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle (v02.193), product code, tests, and receipts.

OPERATOR ORDER (DEC-007, 2026-06-12): NO scaffolding, templates, mock-ups, or vibecoding; every feature must be working AND tested in runtime (executed vitest/Playwright/real-PG proof per feature, not status text). A feature without an executed runtime proof is NOT done.
scope.expected_behavior: Operator sees outline/status/save/go-to-line/link-open behave exactly as VS Code-class chrome on real documents.
acceptance_criteria: Each named parity row demonstrably works in runtime (Playwright per row).
Outline reflects live doc structure; status bar reflects live cursor/language/save state.
Link click opens the linked doc/surface in-app; broken link -> typed visible error.
Adversarial self-review recorded per the WP loop; findings fixed before evidence write.
verification: pnpm vitest run green; tsc --noEmit clean; eslint clean.
Offline Playwright runtime proof for each named capability (zero external network requests).
handoff_requirements: List files changed, tests run with exact names/counts, commits.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence and Playwright artifacts produced.
List remaining risks and any honestly-documented deviations (e.g., prose multi-cursor approach).

evidence: RE-IMPLEMENTED on feat/WP-KERNEL-009 (commit ec95bac9, 16 files app/+tests, zero .GOV). NOTE: the prior wtc-kernel-009-mt245 attempt was orphaned/uncommitted and NEVER integrated or runtime-proven (its self-set completed_by + rich_editor_roundtrip evidence were false); its cherry-pick fold conflicted with the 7 editor MTs, so the chrome was rebuilt fresh on the current tree, integrated with transclusion/Monaco-debug-gutter/source-control.

runtime proof: offline Playwright tests/dependency_policy/editor_workbench_chrome.spec.ts PASS (1 test, real browser, network cut, PNG captured + verified 1280x868) proving all 5 parity rows function: ED-NAV-004 outline (live heading tree + click-to-scroll moving the real selection); ED-WB-007 status bar (live word count, focused Monaco code-block language, authority dirty->saved); ED-NAV-006 go-to-line (out-of-range -> typed visible error); EXT-SAVE-001 Mod-s -> onSaveRequested (save count + state flip); EXT-NAV-LINK-001 (resolvable wp link -> kind:wp no error; unresolvable -> typed visible error)

EXT-NAV-LINK-001 resolution extracted into a pure exported resolveHsLinkTarget() (link_navigation.ts) called by BOTH App.tsx and the harness, so the browser proof exercises the real code (not a copy); link_navigation.test.ts (4) + editor_chrome.test.ts (4) cover it; vitest 13 PASS total; tsc clean; eslint clean over the 16 MT-245 files; check:portable-artifacts PASS 6/6 (no two-'..' artifact-root overreach reintroduced)

VALIDATION CAVEAT: the offline EXT-NAV-LINK-001 proof asserts typed-target resolution + the typed-error path, not the full in-app pane-focus (needs the live backend; App.tsx uses the same resolveHsLinkTarget, covered by the App vitest suite). Pre-existing act() warnings + unrelated pre-existing eslint errors elsewhere in app/ are out of MT-245 scope.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-245 closes five editor-chrome parity rows (ED-NAV-004 outline, ED-WB-007 status bar, ED-NAV-006 go-to-line, EXT-SAVE-001 Mod-s save, EXT-NAV-LINK-001 typed link nav) on the real integrated RichTextEditor. The code is genuine VS Code-class chrome wired through the actual Tiptap/Monaco surfaces, with no placeholders/escapes and forbidden tech absent. Runtime proof is real: offline Playwright in a real Chromium with the network cut, plus vitest over the real editor.
findings: RichTextEditor.tsx:795-1127 renders the live workbench chrome from the real editor: buildEditorOutline(editor.state.doc) drives the outline nav (lines 924-967), with outline clicks calling editor.commands.setTextSelection(item.selectionPos)+scrollIntoView (954-959) - a real selection move, not a label. The ED-WB-007 status bar (1104-1127) is fed by buildEditorStatus over the real doc + live Monaco cursor.

editor_chrome.ts:75-198 contains pure, real model helpers (buildEditorOutline, buildEditorStatus, findFocusedCodeBlock, codeLineRange, countDocumentWords). No stubs; computed over the actual ProseMirror doc shape. editor_chrome.test.ts uses a real Tiptap Editor with buildHandshakeEditorExtensions, not mocks.

ED-NAV-006 go-to-line is real: RichTextEditor.tsx:631-657 runGoToLine resolves the focused code block, computes codeLineRange, and calls revealCodeBlockRange (code_block_find_registry.ts:35) which drives the real Monaco instance.setSelection/revealRangeInCenterIfOutsideViewport (MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx:139-153). Out-of-range -> typed visible error (644-646).
EXT-SAVE-001 is real: RichTextEditor.tsx:709-714 swallows Mod-s (no browser dialog) and fires onSaveRequested, global even from inside Monaco code blocks.

EXT-NAV-LINK-001 is genuinely integrated, not harness-only: link_navigation.ts:70-87 resolveHsLinkTarget is a pure exported resolver; HsLinkView.tsx:109 dispatchHsLinkNavigate fires on real chip click; App.tsx:1294-1312 consumes the same resolver and maps each resolved kind to real pane actions (openWorkspaceDocument etc.), with unresolvable -> typed visible error (1300-1307). The offline harness (editor_workbench_chrome_harness.tsx:99-112) exercises the SAME resolver, so the browser proof hits real code.

ED-WB-007 Monaco language/cursor bridge is real: MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx:105-121 dispatches MONACO_CODE_BLOCK_STATUS_EVENT on onDidFocusEditorText/onDidBlurEditorText/onDidChangeCursorPosition (215-224); RichTextEditor.tsx:450-470 consumes it to drive the status bar language/Ln/Col.

Runtime proof is real and appropriate to a GUI MT: tests/dependency_policy/editor_workbench_chrome.spec.ts drives a real Chromium against the built dist-harness, aborts every non-loopback request at the browser network layer (lines 93-101), asserts all 5 rows function (real selection move, live word count, typescript code language, out-of-range go-to-line error, save-count flip, resolvable wp link -> kind:wp, ghost link -> visible typed error), and asserts zero external requests (204). Matches the contract's named proof (offline Playwright per capability).

sub_rule_2 = NA: this is a UI-chrome/projection MT (constraint: 'UI artifacts are projections only'); it touches no durable PostgreSQL/EventLedger table/column. The contract's verification names offline Playwright + vitest, which is what was produced. Spec 10.2 (Monaco Editor Experience, VS Code-like model, lazy-load with no runtime network fetches) aligns with the offline proof.

sub_rule_3 = PASS: contract lifecycle.completed_by is null and status is READY_FOR_VALIDATION (not self-signed COMPLETED). No forbidden tech (SQLite/Elasticsearch/Docker/CDN) appears in the MT-245 files. Aside (non-blocking): the contract still cites spec bundle v02.193/v02.192 anchors while current is v02.194 - expected paperwork drift, not a defect.
evidence_checked: wtc-kernel-009 git show --stat ec95bac9 (16 files, all app/+tests, zero .GOV)
git grep for escapes/placeholders/forbidden-tech across MT-245 files (none reachable)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger and/or offline Playwright where the contract names durable state or GUI behavior.
